title: Hieracium iteophyllum
intro: Hieracium iteophyllum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its hairy leaves and bright yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium iteophyllum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium iteophyllum is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, which may be used to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Hieracium iteophyllum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to South Africa. The genus Hieracium is commonly known as hawkweed, a group of plants that are often found in wet meadows and rocky areas. The classification of Hieracium iteophyllum is a part of the plant's taxonomy, which is a system used to identify and categorize living organisms. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it shares characteristics with other daisy-like plants.
